item.title: The Power of MLD Massage: Your Path to Optimal Post-Surgical Recovery
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we understand that recovery from surgery isn't just about healing – it's about returning to your life feeling better than ever. That's why we're excited to share our comprehensive approach to post-surgical recovery, combining our expert Manual Lymphatic Drainage (MLD) massage therapy with our newest innovative treatment: specialized microcurrent therapy.

Understanding MLD: Nature's Healing Highway

Manual Lymphatic Drainage isn't just another massage technique – it's a sophisticated, scientifically-proven method that works with your body's natural healing systems. Our certified MLD therapists at Health Pointe Jacksonville use the renowned Vodder technique, considered the gold standard in lymphatic drainage therapy. Once your surgical recovery progresses we will then incorporate further advanced care into your treatment to further reduce adhesions with Graston/GuaSha, and introduce castor oil pack treatments to really accelerate our healing journey This gentle yet powerful treatment helps: - Reduce post-surgical swelling and inflammation - Speed up healing time - Minimize scarring - Enhance immune system function - Decrease pain and discomfort - Improve range of motion

Expert Care from Certified Specialists

Our massage therapy team brings decades of hands-on experience to every treatment, with specialized certification in post-surgical MLD therapy. That advanced training means you're in skilled, knowledgeable hands — practitioners who truly understand the body's healing process and know how to support it every step of the way.

Introducing Our Latest Innovation: Microcurrent Therapy

We're thrilled to announce the addition of cutting-edge microcurrent therapy to our post-surgical recovery program. When combined with MLD massage, this revolutionary treatment takes your healing journey to the next level.

 The Power of Microcurrent Treatment

This gentle electrical stimulation works at a cellular level to: - Accelerate tissue repair - Enhance protein synthesis - Stimulate ATP production (your cells' energy source) - Improve circulation - Reduce inflammation - Decrease scar tissue formation - Provide natural pain relief

The Perfect Partnership: MLD + Microcurrent

When combined, these two powerful therapies create a synergistic effect that can significantly improve your post-surgical recovery. The MLD massage opens and stimulates your lymphatic system, while the microcurrent therapy energizes your cells and promotes deeper healing. This dynamic duo helps: - Reduce recovery time - Minimize post-surgical complications - Improve outcome appearance - Enhance overall healing - Provide greater comfort during recovery - Support long-term tissue health

Your Customized Recovery Journey

At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we understand that every surgery and every patient is unique. That's why we create personalized treatment plans that adapt to your specific needs and recovery goals. Our experienced team will guide you through each step of your healing journey, ensuring you receive the perfect combination of therapies for optimal results. Manual Lymphatic Drainage (MLD) massage is a gentle yet powerful therapy often used to support post-surgical recovery by reducing swelling, accelerating healing, and enhancing immune function. But MLD’s benefits extend far beyond surgery—it’s also a therapeutic tool for managing lymphedema and related conditions such as sports injuries, sinus congestion, migraines, TMJ dysfunction, mastitis, menstrual cramps, post-breast cancer surgery swelling, arthritic inflammation, and fibromyalgia flares. Read more here

Getting Started

Ready to experience the transformative benefits of our post-surgical recovery program? Schedule your initial consultation today. We'll evaluate your specific needs and design a treatment plan that helps you heal faster and more completely. Whether you're healing from surgery or managing a chronic condition, MLD can be a soothing and effective addition to your wellness plan. Ready to feel lighter and more balanced? Call us at 904-448-0046 or visit https://hpjax.com/ to schedule your MLD session today.   A note on initial MLD treatments: An initial consultation is required for all new MLD patients to evaluate and customize a proper care plan for post-surgical recovery. Our team will work closely with your surgeon to ensure the best possible care coordination. A note on the MLD we provide: Our clinic specializes exclusively in Vodder MLD therapy — the gold standard in lymphatic drainage. We do not offer Brazilian MLD for post-surgical fluid removal or body contouring. If you're unsure which type of MLD is right for your recovery, we're happy to help you find the best fit    

Would you like to learn more about our other innovative treatments and therapies? Explore our website  or contact our caring team today. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we're dedicated to supporting your complete healing journey with the most advanced and effective natural therapies available.

#PostSurgicalRecovery #MLD #Wellness #Jacksonville #NaturalHealing #Recovery

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: MLD massage supports post-surgical recovery by reducing swelling, accelerating healing, and enhancing immune function.

item.title: The Transformative Benefits of Prenatal & Postpartum Massage: Your Guide to Supported Motherhood
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
The journey of motherhood is one of life's most profound experiences – and one that transforms your body in remarkable ways. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we believe that every mother deserves expert, nurturing support throughout this incredible journey. Let's explore how our specialized prenatal and postpartum massage therapy can enhance your pregnancy experience and support your fourth trimester recovery.

The Magic of Prenatal Massage

Pregnancy is a time of wonder, but it also brings unique physical challenges. As your body creates and nurtures new life, it undergoes tremendous changes that can lead to discomfort. Our specialized prenatal massage therapy addresses these challenges while honoring the sacred nature of your pregnancy journey.

Expert Care You Can Trust

At Health Pointe Jacksonville, our prenatal massage team brings decades of specialized experience to your care. Our lead therapist Denise, with 29 years of expertise, and Yaira, with over 20 years of practice, hold advanced certifications in prenatal massage. This specialized training means they understand the intricate needs of your changing body, including: - Safe positioning techniques for each trimester - Proper pressure points and areas to avoid - Specific needs for high-risk pregnancies - Techniques to support optimal fetal positioning - Methods to relieve pregnancy-specific discomforts

Benefits Throughout Your Pregnancy

Research shows that regular prenatal massage can significantly improve your pregnancy experience. Our clients report: - Decreased back pain and sciatica - Reduced swelling in hands and feet - Better sleep quality - Lower stress and anxiety levels - Fewer headaches - Improved circulation - Enhanced emotional wellbeing - Greater sense of connection with their baby

The Fourth Trimester: A Time for Healing

The postpartum period, often called the fourth trimester, is a crucial time of healing and adjustment. Traditional cultures worldwide recognize the first 40 days after birth as a sacred window requiring special care and support. Our postpartum massage therapy honors this wisdom while incorporating modern understanding of postpartum recovery.

When to Begin Postpartum Massage

We recommend beginning postpartum massage: - 1-2 weeks after uncomplicated vaginal delivery (or as soon as you are ready) - 6 weeks post-cesarean delivery (with provider clearance) - Once you feel ready to receive therapeutic touch

Supporting Your Recovery Journey

Postpartum massage offers specific benefits for new mothers: - Speeds physical recovery - Reduces postpartum swelling - Eases breastfeeding-related muscle tension - Helps restore proper posture - Supports hormone regulation - Improves sleep quality - Reduces anxiety and baby blues - Provides crucial self-care time - Supports emotional processing of birth experience

Your Customized Treatment Experience

Every pregnancy and postpartum journey is unique, which is why we create individualized treatment plans for each mother. Your session begins with a thorough consultation to understand your specific needs and concerns.

What to Expect

During your 50 or 80-minute session, you'll experience: - A peaceful, private treatment room - Specialized positioning for optimal comfort - Safe, pregnancy-specific massage techniques - Unscented oils that respect your sensitive nose - Gentle, effective pressure suited to your needs - Expert care from certified specialists

Safety First

We maintain the highest standards of safety for prenatal and postpartum massage: - Required healthcare provider clearance for high-risk pregnancies - Specialized protocols for different stages of pregnancy - Careful attention to positioning and pressure - Regular communication about comfort levels - Coordination with your healthcare team when needed
The Importance of Self-Care
In the whirlwind of pregnancy and new motherhood, taking time for self-care isn't just a luxury – it's a necessity. Regular massage therapy can provide the physical and emotional support you need to navigate this transformative time with greater ease and joy.

Ready to Begin Your Supported Journey?

Whether you're in your first trimester or navigating the fourth trimester, our expert team is here to support your wellbeing. Contact Health Pointe Jacksonville today to schedule your prenatal or postpartum massage session. Let us help you embrace this beautiful journey with more comfort, confidence, and peace.  

*For appointments or questions about our prenatal and postpartum massage services, call 904-448-0046 or book online through our website.

item.title: A Natural Approach to Hormone Balance
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
Are you tired of feeling like your hormones are on a roller coaster? At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we understand that hormone imbalances can affect every aspect of your life - from your energy and mood to your skin and fertility. While acupuncture is a powerful tool for hormone regulation, we're excited to share some gentle, natural approaches you can implement at home to support your hormonal health journey. Nature has provided us with incredible tools for hormone balance, and two of our favorite complementary approaches are seed cycling and targeted food therapy. These ancient wisdom-backed methods work in harmony with your body's natural rhythms, providing the essential nutrients needed for optimal hormone production and metabolism. Think of seed cycling as your monthly hormone-balancing ritual, where specific seeds are consumed during different phases of your cycle to support healthy estrogen and progesterone levels. When combined with intentional food choices that naturally boost or modulate hormone levels, you're giving your body powerful tools to find its balance. The beauty of these approaches lies in their simplicity and accessibility. Whether you're dealing with PMS, irregular cycles, fertility challenges, or navigating perimenopause, integrating these natural strategies alongside your acupuncture treatments can amplify your results and empower you to take an active role in your hormone health. Let's explore how you can harness these natural approaches to create harmony in your hormonal landscape...  

What is Seed Cycling?

  Seed cycling is an emerging natural method for balancing hormones by incorporating specific seeds into your diet during different phases of your menstrual cycle. It’s a simple yet powerful way to support your body's natural rhythms, promoting hormonal harmony and improving overall well-being. Seed cycling involves consuming specific types of seeds during the follicular and luteal phases of your menstrual cycle. The nutrients in these seeds are believed to support estrogen and progesterone production, helping to regulate your cycle, ease PMS symptoms, and improve reproductive health. This practice is rooted in the idea that different seeds can influence hormone levels due to their lignans, essential fatty acids, and nutrient profiles.

How Does Seed Cycling Work?

Your menstrual cycle is divided into two main phases:
  1. Follicular Phase (Day 1 to Ovulation) This phase begins on the first day of your period and lasts until ovulation. During this time, estrogen levels gradually rise to support the maturation of the egg.

    Seeds to Eat:

    • Flaxseeds: Rich in lignans, which can help modulate estrogen levels and promote healthy estrogen metabolism.
    • Pumpkin Seeds: High in zinc, which supports progesterone production for the luteal phase.
    Daily Dose: 1 tablespoon each of ground flaxseeds and pumpkin seeds. Ground seeds are easier to digest and maximize nutrient absorption.
  2. Luteal Phase (Ovulation to Day 28) After ovulation, progesterone becomes the dominant hormone to prepare the body for a potential pregnancy or to support the menstrual process.

    Seeds to Eat:

    • Sunflower Seeds: Rich in selenium, which supports liver detoxification and hormone metabolism.
    • Sesame Seeds: Contain lignans that support progesterone production and balance estrogen levels.
    Daily Dose: 1 tablespoon each of sunflower seeds and sesame seeds.

Beyond Seed Cycling: Supporting Hormone Balance Through Food Therapy

While seed cycling offers a structured approach to hormone balance, your everyday food choices also play a crucial role in maintaining optimal hormone levels. Just as certain seeds support different phases of your cycle, specific foods can help stimulate your body's natural hormone production and create an environment for hormonal harmony.

Let's explore how to naturally boost progesterone through mindful eating. While foods don't necessarily contain progesterone, some may help stimulate the body's production of progesterone. Incorporating the following into your diet can be a great way to encourage natural hormone balance:

How to Naturally Boost Progesterone

While foods don’t necessarily contain progesterone, some may help stimulate the body’s production of progesterone. Incorporating the following into your diet can be a great way to encourage natural hormone balance:

Foods to Help Boost Progesterone Production:

  • Beans
  • Broccoli
  • Brussels sprouts
  • Cabbage
  • Cauliflower
  • Kale
  • Nuts
  • Pumpkin
  • Spinach
  • Whole grains
Foods That May Lower Estrogen Levels to Improve Progesterone Balance:
  • Bananas
  • Cabbage
  • Shellfish
  • Walnuts
These foods work by either promoting progesterone production or lowering estrogen, which can help balance the ratio of progesterone to estrogen in the body.

Foods That Help Boost Estrogen During the Follicular Phase

During the follicular phase, supporting estrogen levels can promote optimal egg maturation and preparation for ovulation. In addition to flaxseeds and pumpkin seeds, include the following foods:

Estrogen-Boosting Foods:

  • Soy products (tofu, tempeh, edamame)
  • Chickpeas
  • Lentils
  • Berries
  • Apples
  • Carrots
  • Beets
  • Pomegranates
  • Sesame seeds
  • Alfalfa sprouts
These foods are rich in phytoestrogens, which mimic natural estrogen in the body and help support healthy levels during this phase.

Benefits of Seed Cycling and Nutritional Support

  • Balances Hormones: Supports estrogen and progesterone levels at appropriate times in the cycle.
  • Relieves PMS Symptoms: Reduces bloating, mood swings, and cramps.
  • Supports Fertility: Encourages a regular cycle and hormonal balance, beneficial for conception.
  • Improves Skin Health: Balanced hormones can lead to clearer skin.
  • Supports Menopause: Helps alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and mood changes.

How to Incorporate Seed Cycling and Foods into Your Routine

  1. Choose Fresh, Organic Ingredients: Use raw, unsalted seeds and fresh, whole foods for maximum nutrient benefits.
  2. Be Creative: Add seeds and hormone-supporting foods to smoothies, salads, soups, or baked goods.
  3. Stay Consistent: Include these foods daily to achieve long-term benefits.

Who Can Benefit from Seed Cycling and Nutritional Support?

  • Women with irregular menstrual cycles.
  • Those experiencing PMS or hormonal acne.
  • Women going through perimenopause or menopause.
  • Anyone looking to support overall hormonal health naturally.
Embrace the power of seed cycling and a hormone-supportive diet as a gentle, natural way to balance your hormones and improve your well-being. Your body will thank you!

item.title: Revolutionizing Pelvic Health: Introducing Our New Specialty Program
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
We're thrilled to announce the launch of our specialized HPJ Pelvic Floor Acupuncture Program, beginning January 2025. This innovative treatment integrates evidence-based techniques with cutting-edge medical understanding to address one of the most overlooked aspects of health: the pelvic floor Think of your pelvic floor as the foundation of a house. When this foundation is strong, everything above it functions smoothly. When it's compromised, the effects ripple throughout the entire structure. Yet surprisingly, many people live with pelvic floor dysfunction without realizing there's a solution.

Pelvic Floor Stats:

  • Studies show that 35% of those with pelvic pain also presented with irritable bowel syndrome.
  • Another showed that 61% of women with bladder pain syndrome and 50% of those with endometriosis also have comorbid pelvic pain.
  • Pelvic pain has a prevalence of >25% in women and >15% in men, and up to 50% of cases are undiagnosed
  • 20% of women and 8% of men report experiencing pain during intercourse, often linked to pelvic floor dysfunction. And these are just the people who were willing to talk about it…. Pro Tip: Pain during sex is not normal
  • The annual cost of pelvic health care in the United States is estimated to be around $16 Billion, with a significant portion attributed to the treatment of urinary incontinence
  • Approximately 46% of patients with chronic pelvic pain have reported experiencing sexual or physical abuse, including sexual assault or domestic violence, according to studies conducted in pelvic pain clinics
  • The Vagus nerve is the longest nerve in the body. In fact research shows fibers going all the way from the cranium to the pelvis. Which is why  patients with pelvic floor muscle dysfunction are 94.6% more likely to also experience jaw dysfunction.
  • There is a direct correlation between our digestive tract and our pelvic floor. This means that there is dysfunction in either the digestive tract or the floor or both, we are very likely producing less serotonin than optimal. 90% of serotonin is released from the gut (Serotonin is linked to a positive mood, great sleep, good memory capacity, and reduced stress).

Understanding Pelvic Floor Health

Your pelvic floor isn't just a set of muscles – it's a complex network that supports your core, affects your posture, influences your breathing, and impacts everything from bladder control to sexual function. When these muscles become too tight or too weak, the effects can be far-reaching and significantly impact your quality of life.

The Magic of Acupuncture for Pelvic Health

Acupuncture offers a unique approach to pelvic floor care.

Through precise trigger point needling, dry needling, and targeted neuromuscular techniques, we can:

  • Release deep muscle tension and fascial restrictions
  • Improve local blood circulation and tissue oxygenation
  • Reduce inflammation and neural sensitivity
  • Support healthy muscle function and tone
  • Facilitate neuromuscular re-education
  • Support natural tissue healing processes

 Who Can Benefit?

Our program is especially valuable for:
  • New Mothers Pregnancy and childbirth fundamentally change your pelvic floor. Whether you delivered vaginally or via C-section, acupuncture can support your postpartum recovery, helping restore strength and function while addressing common issues like incontinence or diastasis recti.
  • Menopausal Women Hormonal changes during menopause can affect pelvic floor strength and tissue health. Our treatments help manage symptoms like vaginal dryness, frequent urination, and pelvic organ prolapse while supporting overall hormonal balance.
  • Active Adults Whether you're a runner, yogi, or weekend warrior, pelvic floor health is crucial for performance and injury prevention. Our program helps maintain optimal muscle function and prevents exercise-related pelvic floor issues.
  • Office Workers Prolonged sitting can wreak havoc on your pelvic floor. Our treatments address the effects of sedentary lifestyles, improving posture and reducing discomfort.
  • Men with Chronic Pelvic Pain Syndrome (CPPS) or Chronic Prostatitis Many men don't realize that pelvic floor dysfunction can significantly impact their daily lives. While often overlooked, the pelvic floor plays a crucial role in men's health, affecting everything from athletic performance to intimate wellness.
Anyone Experiencing:
  • Bladder control issues
  • Lower back pain
  • Hip discomfort
  • Sexual dysfunction
  • Pelvic pressure or pain
  • Digestive problems
  • Core weakness
  • Pain in genitals
  • Infertility
  • Painful periods

Why Add This to Your Wellness Plan?

Prevention is always better than cure. Many people wait until symptoms become unbearable before seeking help. By incorporating pelvic floor acupuncture into your wellness routine, you can: 1. Address minor issues before they become major problems 2. Maintain optimal pelvic health throughout life changes 3. Improve your overall physical performance 4. Enhance your body awareness and control 5. Support long-term reproductive and sexual health 6. Build a stronger foundation for whole-body wellness

 Our Unique Approach

Our program doesn't just treat symptoms – we look at your whole health picture. Treatment care planning for pelvic floor dysfunction includes: - Comprehensive evaluation - Customized treatment plan - Lifestyle recommendations - Exercise guidance - Progress tracking - Integration with your existing healthcare team

 Your Next Steps

Ready to transform your pelvic health? Here's how to get started: 1. Schedule a consultation for January 2025 2. Receive a personalized assessment 3. Begin your journey to better pelvic health 4. Experience the benefits of integrated care  

Don't wait for symptoms to worsen. Invest in your foundation of health today. Contact us to secure your spot in our new Pelvic Floor Acupuncture Program.

*Limited spaces available for our January 2025 program launch. Early scheduling is recommended.*

item.title: Steam or Smoke? How Two Ancient Rituals Are Transforming Modern Women’s Health
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
Let’s talk about the pelvic bowl and how to improve pelvic health. For centuries, women have intuitively cared for this sacred space using herbs, heat, and ritual—long before modern medicine caught on to the idea that our reproductive health is intimately tied to our mental clarity, emotional balance, and overall vitality. Now, two time-tested therapies are gaining modern attention for their powerful effects on hormonal harmony, fertility, postpartum healing, and pelvic pain relief: vaginal steaming and moxibustion vaginal smoking. Both are rooted in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and other global healing systems, and both are making a bold, beautiful comeback—not because they’re trendy, but because they work. So how do you know which is right for you? Let’s break it down.

First, Let’s Take the Weird Out of It

Yes, these treatments involve your vulva and warming herbs. No, they’re not fringe or “woo.” In TCM, the uterus and pelvic region are seen as centers of energy, creativity, and emotional regulation. When this area becomes cold, stagnant, or depleted, women may experience symptoms like painful periods, hormonal imbalances, digestive issues, low libido, and even fertility challenges. Both vaginal steaming and moxibustion vaginal smoking aim to restore warmth and flow to this space—but they do it differently.

Vaginal Steaming: A Gentle Herbal Embrace

Vaginal steaming (or “yoni steaming”) is a gentle ritual where you sit cloaked over a bowl of warm, herb-infused steam that softly rises to the vaginal and uterine tissues. Think of it as a pelvic sauna—intimate, deeply relaxing, and surprisingly transformative.

How it works:

  • Steam carries herbal actives (like mugwort, motherwort, chamomile, and rosemary) to the vaginal and uterine area
  • The warmth opens pores and soft tissues, inviting in circulation and clearing out stagnation
  • You emerge feeling lighter, softer, more in tune with your body

When to try vaginal steaming:

  • You’re recovering from childbirth or a miscarriage
  • You experience painful periods, blood clots, or irregular cycles
  • You struggle with mild yeast infections or dryness
  • You want to reconnect with your body in a soothing, spa-like ritual

Who should skip it:

  • If you’re pregnant, actively bleeding, or have an infection
  • If you’re highly sensitive to heat or steam

Moxibustion Vaginal Smoking: Deep, Dry Heat for Pelvic Qi

This lesser-known sister therapy offers a deeper, more penetrating heat than steam. Using the dried herb mugwort (Ai Ye), moxibustion involves burning the herb in a safe, controlled vessel and allowing the warm, aromatic smoke to gently waft toward the pelvic region. While the idea of “smoking your lady parts” may raise eyebrows, rest assured: this is an elegant, therapeutic ritual rooted in centuries of clinical use. And it’s a game-changer for women who need serious pelvic support.

How it works:

  • The dry heat penetrates deeper layers of tissue
  • It warms the uterus, tonifies Qi, and clears cold stagnation
  • It’s especially helpful for those experiencing fatigue, prolapse, or low reproductive energy

When to try moxibustion vaginal smoking:

  • You’ve been told you have a “cold uterus” or poor blood flow
  • You’re recovering from prolapse or cesarean birth
  • You’re trying to conceive and want to support fertile uterine warmth
  • You experience chronic pelvic pain or fatigue
  • You prefer dry heat over steam

Who should skip it:

  • If you’re pregnant
  • If you’re sensitive to smoke or have respiratory issues
  • If you have active infections

How Do They Compare?

Vaginal Steaming Moxibustion Vaginal Smoking
Modality Moist heat (herbal steam) Dry heat (herbal smoke)
Vibe Gentle, spa-like, hydrating Deep, focused, warming from within
Best for Menstrual cramps, mild infections, postpartum Prolapse, fertility, chronic pelvic pain
Penetration Surface-level, soft tissues Deep tissue and energy channels
Avoid if Pregnant, bleeding, active infection Pregnant, sensitive to smoke

Still Not Sure Which to Try First?

Sometimes your body will tell you: if you’re feeling dry, tender, or emotionally overwhelmed, steaming can offer that soft, nourishing reset. If you’re feeling cold, depleted, or disconnected from your center, moxibustion may be the heat your system is craving. Better yet, work with a practitioner who can guide you. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we specialize in women’s health, fertility, and postpartum care using the wisdom of Traditional Chinese Medicine. We offer treatments customized to your cycle, constitution, and goals.

The Bottom Line: This Isn’t a Trend. It’s a Return.

These therapies aren’t new. They’re ancient solutions that speak to modern women’s needs: less stress, better flow, deeper connection to self. They’re not about fixing you—they’re about bringing you back to yourself. ✨ Your body already knows how to heal. These rituals just help clear the path.

Book a Session at Health Pointe Jacksonville

Ready to explore vaginal steaming or moxibustion vaginal smoking in a safe, professional setting? 📞 Call us: 904-448-0046 🌐 Book online: https://hpjax.com/ Reclaim your cycle. Nourish your womb. Connect with your center.

item.title: February: Nurturing the Heart with Acupuncture and Massage
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
February: Nurturing the Heart with Acupuncture and Massage

The Month of Love & Heart Health

February is all about love, connection, and self-care—but it’s not just about romantic love. It’s also Heart Health Month, making it the perfect time to focus on your physical and emotional well-being. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we believe that true wellness starts from within. Whether you're looking to deepen your connection with a partner, reduce stress, or give yourself some well-deserved self-care, acupuncture and massage therapy offer powerful ways to nourish the heart—physically and emotionally.

The Heart Connection in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM)

In Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), the Heart is more than just an organ—it’s the ruler of emotions, joy, and vitality. The Heart governs: ❤️ Circulation & Blood Flow – keeping the body energized and balanced ❤️ Emotional Well-being – housing the Shen (spirit), which affects mood and mental clarity ❤️ Connection & Love – influencing how we experience love, joy, and relationships When the Heart Qi (energy) is imbalanced, symptoms can appear such as: ❌ Anxiety, restlessness, or trouble sleeping ❌ Poor circulation, cold hands and feet ❌ Heart palpitations or emotional instability ❌ Fatigue and lack of joy The good news? Acupuncture and massage can restore balance to the Heart, helping you feel more connected, grounded, and full of vitality.

Acupuncture for Heart Health & Emotional Well-being

Acupuncture is a gentle yet powerful way to improve circulation, reduce stress, and regulate emotions. By targeting specific points, we can: ✔ Support Cardiovascular Health – Improve circulation and regulate blood pressure ✔ Calm the Mind & Reduce Stress – Balance the nervous system and ease anxiety ✔ Enhance Emotional Well-being – Promote feelings of love, joy, and emotional stability ✔ Improve Sleep Quality – A calm heart leads to restful sleep and mental clarity 💖 Best Acupuncture Points for Heart Balance:
  • Heart 7 (Shenmen) – The “Spirit Gate” for calming the mind and easing anxiety
  • Pericardium 6 (Neiguan) – Reduces stress, promotes circulation, and relieves chest tightness
  • Ren 17 (Shanzhong) – The “Sea of Tranquility” for opening the heart and emotional healing

Massage Therapy: The Gift of Touch & Connection

Massage is more than just relaxation—it’s a powerful tool for physical and emotional well-being. Studies show that massage can: ✔ Increase Oxytocin ("Love Hormone") – Enhancing connection and emotional bonding ✔ Reduce Stress Hormones (Cortisol & Adrenaline) – Helping you feel calmer and more present ✔ Improve Circulation & Heart Function – Supporting overall cardiovascular health ✔ Relieve Muscle Tension – Easing tension held in the chest, shoulders, and back This Valentine’s season, consider sharing the healing power of touch with a couples’ massage or a self-care massage session—because love starts with self-care.

Self-Love, Connection & Heart-Centered Wellness

Whether you’re celebrating Valentine’s Day with a partner or embracing self-love, February is the perfect time to nurture your heart. 💕 Ways to Show Love to Your Heart This Month: ✔ Book an acupuncture session to promote balance and relaxation ✔ Treat yourself or a loved one to a massage therapy session ✔ Spend time with those who bring you joy and laughter ✔ Practice deep breathing, gratitude, and mindfulness ✔ Enjoy heart-healthy foods like dark chocolate, berries, and green tea

February Specials at Health Pointe Jacksonville

To celebrate Heart Health Month & Valentine’s Day, we’re offering: ❤️ Heart-Opening Acupuncture Sessions – Balance emotions & improve circulation ❤️ Massage Therapy Gift Certificates – A perfect gift for self-care or a loved one ❤️ Couples’ Massage Packages – Share relaxation and connection with your partner 📞 Call us at: 904-448-0046 🌐 Book online: www.hpjax.com This February, let’s nurture the heart, cultivate love, and embrace wellness—together. 💖

item.title: Endometriosis Awareness Month: Breaking Through the Silence Barrier
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
March is Endometriosis Awareness Month, a time to shed light on a condition that affects 1 in 10 women of reproductive age worldwide. Yet, despite its prevalence, endometriosis remains one of the most misunderstood and underdiagnosed conditions in women’s health. Far too many women suffer in silence, dismissed by doctors, misunderstood by loved ones, and told their pain is “just part of being a woman.”

Endometriosis: More Than Just “Bad Periods”

Endometriosis occurs when tissue similar to the lining of the uterus grows outside the uterine cavity, responding to hormonal changes just as it would inside the uterus. This leads to chronic inflammation, scarring, and excruciating pain that can severely impact daily life. Symptoms often include: 🔸 Debilitating menstrual cramps 🔸 Chronic pelvic pain 🔸 Pain during or after sex 🔸 Gastrointestinal distress (bloating, nausea, IBS-like symptoms) 🔸 Fatigue and immune dysfunction 🔸 Fertility challenges For some women, endometriosis pain is constant, not just something that flares up during menstruation. And yet, they are told, over and over again, that painful periods are “normal”—a dangerous and completely false myth.

Why Are Women Still Not Being Believed?

The real tragedy of endometriosis is not just the pain—it’s the years of medical gaslighting that so many women endure. The average diagnosis takes 7-10 years, during which women are often misdiagnosed with anxiety, IBS, or simply told to take ibuprofen and “tough it out.” Even worse, dismissive comments from people in power continue to add to this harm. Australian radio host Marty Sheargold recently claimed on air that “endometriosis is not a real thing. It’s made up.” Imagine the damage that statement does to the millions of women already struggling to be taken seriously. Endometriosis is real. It is not normal to experience debilitating pain. And women deserve better than being told to “just deal with it.”

Acupuncture: A Holistic Approach to Endometriosis Relief

While there is no cure for endometriosis, a comprehensive treatment plan can help manage symptoms. Many women find relief through acupuncture, a key component of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), which works to balance the body, reduce inflammation, and relieve pain naturally. 🌿 Benefits of Acupuncture for Endometriosis: ✅ Pain Reduction – Acupuncture stimulates the release of endorphins, the body’s natural painkillers. ✅ Reduced Inflammation – Studies suggest acupuncture can help lower inflammatory markers in the body. ✅ Hormonal Regulation – Regular sessions may help balance hormones, reducing symptom flare-ups. ✅ Stress & Nervous System Support – Chronic pain is exhausting; acupuncture helps break the pain-stress cycle, promoting deep relaxation. ✅ Improved Blood Flow – Enhanced circulation to the reproductive organs may support healing and lessen pain. Many women report a significant improvement in their quality of life with acupuncture. While every case is unique, an initial series of 8-12 weekly treatments is often recommended, followed by maintenance care.

At-Home Support: 5 Natural Remedies for Managing Endometriosis Pain

Between medical treatments, these natural approaches may provide relief: 🔹 1. Heat Therapy A heating pad or warm bath with Epsom salts can help relax pelvic muscles and improve circulation. 🔹 2. Castor Oil Packs Castor oil is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can be applied to the lower abdomen with a warm compress. 🔹 3. Anti-Inflammatory Supplements
  • Omega-3 Fatty Acids (Krill oil, fish oil)
  • Curcumin (Turmeric extract)
  • N-Acetylcysteine (NAC) – Some studies suggest NAC may slow the growth of endometrial tissue.
🔹 4. Pelvic Floor Therapy & Gentle Stretching Many women with endometriosis develop pelvic floor dysfunction. Working with a pelvic floor physical therapist and practicing gentle stretches (child’s pose, hip openers, diaphragmatic breathing) can ease pain. 🔹 5. Anti-Inflammatory Diet A diet that reduces processed foods, dairy, gluten, and sugar while focusing on whole, nutrient-dense foods may help decrease systemic inflammation.

A Call for Change: We Must Do Better for Women

Endometriosis is a serious medical condition, not just “bad periods.” Women deserve: 💛 Faster diagnoses 💛 Access to better treatments 💛 Doctors who listen and believe them 💛 A world that does not dismiss their pain This Endometriosis Awareness Month, let’s amplify the voices of those suffering. Let’s educate, advocate, and demand change—because no woman should have to fight to be believed. If you or someone you love is struggling with endometriosis, know this: You are not alone. Your pain is real. And you deserve care that truly helps. We are here to help 💛

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Endometriosis Awareness Month is a chance to talk about symptoms, support, and next steps. Learn how to spot signs, advocate for care, and find resources.

item.title: National Infertility Awareness Month: The Power of Self-Care, Support Systems, and Acupressure
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
From April 20 to April 26, we observed National Infertility Awareness Week (NIAW), a week dedicated to raising awareness, providing support, and advocating for improved access to fertility care. The 2025 theme, #ALLinFertility, emphasizes the shared struggles and the strength of those facing infertility. This week is not only an opportunity to highlight the challenges of infertility, but also to focus on the importance of self-care and having a strong support system during this difficult journey.

The Reality of Infertility: 1 in 6 Couples Struggling in Silence

Infertility affects 1 in 6 couples worldwide, a statistic that often remains hidden behind closed doors. Many people suffering from infertility do so in silence, grappling with the physical, emotional, and financial burden alone. The emotional toll of infertility is profound, not just for those directly affected but also for their families and friends who are often unsure of how to offer support. The emotional journey of infertility can bring feelings of isolation, frustration, grief, and a sense of loss. Couples may feel an overwhelming pressure, facing societal expectations and unspoken stigmas. For family members and friends, watching a loved one endure the challenges of infertility without knowing how to help can be heart-wrenching as well. The support and understanding of those closest to them can make all the difference in coping with these emotional challenges.

The Importance of Self-Care During Infertility

Infertility can feel overwhelming—physically, emotionally, and mentally. Treatments like IVF, hormone injections, and frequent doctor visits can take a toll on the body and the mind. Self-care isn’t just about bubble baths and pampering (though those are nice too); it’s about creating space for yourself to heal, cope, and thrive despite the challenges. Here are some essential self-care tips to support your emotional and physical well-being during infertility:

1. Listen to Your Body

Fertility treatments can be physically demanding. If you're feeling drained, give yourself permission to rest. Whether it's taking a nap, practicing yoga, or simply enjoying quiet moments, listen to what your body needs.

2. Practice Mindfulness and Relaxation

Stress can be a major barrier to fertility. Incorporating mindfulness, meditation, or breathing exercises into your routine can help reduce anxiety, promote mental clarity, and support your emotional health. Even just five minutes a day can make a difference.

3. Nourish Yourself with Whole Foods

Nutrition plays an important role in overall health, including reproductive health. Focus on nutrient-dense, whole foods that fuel your body with the vitamins and minerals it needs. Consider working with a nutritionist who specializes in fertility to tailor your diet to your needs.

4. Celebrate Small Wins

Infertility is a long and sometimes uncertain journey. Celebrate the small victories along the way—whether it’s making it through a tough week of treatments or simply taking a moment to acknowledge your strength. Recognizing your resilience is vital in maintaining a positive mindset.

The Power of Having a Support System

One of the most significant aspects of navigating infertility is having a reliable and compassionate support system. The emotional rollercoaster of infertility can feel isolating, but you don’t have to go through it alone. Here’s why having a support system is essential:

1. Emotional Support

Having friends, family, or support groups to talk to can help alleviate the emotional weight of infertility. Expressing your feelings and being heard by others who understand can provide comfort and reduce feelings of isolation.

2. Practical Help

Sometimes the practical side of fertility treatments—appointments, travel, and logistics—can become overwhelming. Don’t hesitate to lean on loved ones to help with errands, childcare, or even just providing a distraction when you need it.

3. Connecting with Others Who Understand

Joining a support group for those experiencing infertility can create a space to share stories and advice. Connecting with others who are going through similar experiences can offer validation and comfort. Many organizations, such as RESOLVE: The National Infertility Association, offer online and in-person support groups.

4. Encouraging Advocacy

A strong support system can also help you amplify your voice in advocating for change. Whether it’s contacting lawmakers about improving fertility coverage or sharing your story on social media, support from others can fuel your efforts to advocate for better access to fertility care.

Acupressure for Self-Care and Mental Health

Acupressure, a gentle, natural therapy based on traditional Chinese medicine, can be a powerful tool for relieving stress, anxiety, and promoting overall well-being. Here are some key acupressure points to use at home for self-care during infertility:

1. Shen Men (HT7) - The Heart Gate

This point is excellent for calming the mind and reducing anxiety, which is especially beneficial during the emotional rollercoaster of infertility.
  • Location: On the inside of the wrist, in the crease where the hand meets the arm.
  • How to Use: Press gently with your thumb or fingers for 1-2 minutes while breathing deeply and focusing on calming thoughts.

2. Third Eye Point (Yintang)

This point helps relieve tension, reduce stress, and promote a sense of peace and calm.
  • Location: Between the eyebrows, just above the bridge of the nose.
  • How to Use: Use your index finger and middle finger to apply gentle pressure for 2-3 minutes.

3. Spleen 6 (SP6)

This point helps balance hormones, improve circulation to the reproductive organs, and reduce anxiety.
  • Location: On the inside of the lower leg, about 4 finger-widths above the ankle bone.
  • How to Use: Apply steady pressure for 1-2 minutes on each side.

4. Pericardium 6 (PC6) - Inner Gate

This acupressure point helps with nausea, anxiety, and emotional stress, often experienced by those undergoing fertility treatments.
  • Location: On the inside of the forearm, about 2 inches above the wrist crease.
  • How to Use: Apply gentle pressure with your thumb for 1-2 minutes on each side.
 

Final Thoughts: You Are Not Alone

Infertility is a challenging road to walk, but it’s important to remember that you don’t have to walk it alone. By practicing self-care, using acupressure, and leaning on your support system, you can navigate this journey with strength, hope, and resilience. As we observe NIAW 2025, let’s come together to raise awareness, offer support, and advocate for better access to fertility care. To all those facing infertility —remember, you are #ALLinFertility, and your journey matters. #NationalInfertilityAwarenessWeek #NIAW2025 #ALLinFertility #SelfCare #SupportSystem #Acupressure #InfertilityJourney #FertilityAwareness #InfertilitySupport #AdvocateForChange

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: National Infertility Awareness Month support with self-care ideas, building a support system, and simple acupressure tips. A supportive read for TTC. Read more.

item.title: May is Pelvic Health Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
Reclaim Strength, Confidence, and Relief:

Reintroducing Pelvic Floor Acupuncture Care at Health Pointe Jacksonville

In honor of Pelvic Health Awareness Month At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we often hear from patients who’ve tried everything—physical therapy, medications, surgery consults—and still struggle with pelvic floor dysfunction. That’s why we’re proud to reintroduce our specialized Pelvic Floor Program, combining the power of acupuncture, dry needling, and therapeutic bodywork to address pelvic imbalances from the inside out. ✨ Acupuncture is often the last resort—yet yields the best results. ✨ Whether you're navigating chronic pain, postpartum recovery, or performance-related injuries, our evidence-based, integrative approach provides targeted relief with lasting outcomes.

Why Pelvic Floor Acupuncture?

Pelvic floor dysfunction can affect every area of life—from movement and intimacy to digestion, urination, and emotional well-being. Our unique program blends trigger point acupuncture, dry needling, neuromuscular release, and corrective exercises to address both symptoms and their root causes. Benefits include:
  • Reduced pelvic and low back pain
  • Decreased muscle tension and spasms
  • Improved bladder control and continence
  • Enhanced sexual function
  • Greater pelvic strength and stability
  • Core activation and posture support
  • Accelerated post-surgery healing
  • Boosted athletic performance
This isn’t just pain relief—it’s pelvic empowerment.

Who Can Benefit?

Pelvic floor therapy is not “just” for postpartum women. Our patients include:

👩‍🍼 Post-Pregnancy Women

  • Experiencing pelvic pain or pressure
  • Dealing with urinary incontinence
  • Healing from C-section or vaginal birth
  • Managing diastasis recti (abdominal separation)

🧘‍♂️ Active Adults & Athletes

  • Cross Fitters, power lifters or long distance runners with pelvic pain
  • Road cyclists who spend too much time in the saddle
  • Yoga or Pilates practitioners needing pelvic balance
  • Anyone seeking better posture, stability, and recovery

👩‍🦳 Menopausal & Perimenopausal Women

  • Navigating hormonal shifts
  • Managing pelvic organ prolapse
  • Experiencing changes in tissue tone, dryness, or discomfort

🧍‍♂️ Men with Pelvic or Core Concerns

  • Chronic groin pain
  • Post-surgical scar tissue
  • Athletic or occupational strain

What We Treat

Our pelvic health care program addresses a wide range of concerns, including:
  • Chronic pelvic pain
  • Urinary leakage or urgency
  • Constipation and straining
  • Post-surgical scarring or adhesions
  • Low back or hip pain
  • Core weakness or instability
  • Painful intercourse (dyspareunia)
  • Postpartum recovery
  • Sports-related pelvic injury

What to Expect

Every journey starts with a comprehensive evaluation where we listen deeply to your history, symptoms, and goals. Your custom treatment plan may include:
  • Pelvic Floor Acupuncture & Dry Needling To reduce muscle tension, activate healing, and restore balance
  • Trigger Point Release To address muscular dysfunctional holding patterns and reduce referred pain
  • Therapeutic Movement & Postural Training To re-educate the body and support long-term strength
  • At-Home Exercises Tailored routines to reinforce your results between sessions
  • Nervous System Regulation To reduce anxiety, improve sleep, and increase body awareness

Let’s Break the Silence Around Pelvic Health

Pelvic dysfunction doesn’t just affect your body—it affects how you live, love, move, and show up in the world. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we’re creating a safe, compassionate space to heal what’s been holding you back. If you've been told "everything looks normal" but nothing feels normal, we see you. If you’ve felt dismissed, discouraged, or disconnected, we’re here to help reconnect you to your strength and resilience. 📞 Call us at 904-448-0046 💻 Visit www.hpjax.com 📍 Located in Jacksonville, FL
This Pelvic Health Awareness Month, take the first step toward reclaiming your core, your confidence, and your calm.

item.title: Nurture Your Path to Parenthood With Fertility Massage
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
✨ Nurture Your Path to Parenthood: Discover the Power of Fertility Massage  Now Available at Health Pointe Jacksonville

    Your fertility journey deserves every ounce of support—physical, emotional, and energetic. That’s why we’re excited to introduce Fertility Massage, our newest offering designed to harmonize your body, reduce stress, and support your reproductive health naturally. Whether you're trying to conceive naturally or preparing for IUI or IVF, fertility massage can be a powerful ally in optimizing your body for pregnancy.  

💆‍♀️ What is Fertility Massage?

Fertility Massage is not your typical relaxation massage. It’s a gentle, purposeful protocol designed to enhance the function of the reproductive, digestive, and nervous systems while reducing stress and promoting hormonal balance.

Each fertility massage session is a 60-minute therapeutic journey designed to restore balance and optimize reproductive health through a variety of specialized techniques. The treatment often begins with the application of warm castor oil packs over the abdomen, promoting deep detoxification while supporting the breakdown of cysts and fibroids—especially beneficial for those with PCOS or endometriosis. Castor oil’s natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also nourish the gut and womb, laying the foundation for healing. Gentle cranial sacral holds follow, aligning the head and pelvis to open the body’s central energy channel and support hormonal regulation. Many people don't realize there is an innate connection between the jaw and the pelvis. In fact, research shows there is a 94.6% correlation between pelvic pain or dysfunction and TMJ dysfunction.  To further release tension and encourage pelvic openness, this type of massage includes jaw and TMJ release work, addressing the often-overlooked connection between jaw tension and pelvic floor tightness. Pelvic friction techniques are then used to increase circulation to the uterus and ovaries while helping break down adhesions and reduce congestion in the abdominal region. Acupressure along the bladder meridians, particularly through the back and spine, stimulates energetic pathways associated with reproduction and hormonal flow. Finally, the session may end with pelvic rocking, a gentle, rhythmic movement that mimics the motion of dance and promotes relaxation in the hips and lower back—enhancing fertility through subtle but powerful movement.

It is the ultimate addition to your fertility journey, especially if you struggle with painful periods/endo, or recurrent pregnancy loss. It's helpful in preparing for egg retrieval or embryo transfer, or simply wanting to regulate their cycle and reduce stress.

🌿 Benefits of Fertility Massage

1. Improves blood flow to the uterus and ovaries Good circulation is essential for optimal egg quality and a receptive uterine lining. This massage stimulates blood flow, helping nourish reproductive organs with oxygen and nutrients. 2. Supports hormonal balance By balancing the cranial-sacral alignment, and stimulating the digestive system, liver, and lymphatics, fertility massage helps your body more efficiently process and eliminate excess hormones, reducing imbalances that can interfere with conception. 3. Reduces stress and cortisol levels The fertility journey is stressful—and stress can disrupt ovulation and hormone regulation. This deeply relaxing massage helps reset your nervous system and foster emotional wellbeing. 4. Breaks down pelvic adhesions and scar tissue For those with past surgeries, endometriosis, or pelvic tension, fertility massage helps reduce adhesions that may block the fallopian tubes or interfere with uterine alignment. 5. Enhances lymphatic drainage and detoxification By stimulating the lymph system, this treatment supports gentle detox—an important part of preconception care, especially before undergoing fertility treatments. 6. Reconnects you with your womb space This is more than just physical touch—it’s a sacred time to reconnect, nurture, and honor your body. Many women find it emotionally healing, especially after loss or prolonged struggles to conceive.

Whether you’re trying to conceive after a loss, navigating IVF cycles, or looking for a natural fertility boost, this massage gently supports the body’s innate ability to heal and create.

🗓️ When Should I Start Fertility Massage?

Ideally, begin fertility massage at least 1-3 months prior to trying to conceive or starting assisted reproductive treatments like IUI or IVF. This gives your body time to respond, regulate, and restore balance. Treatments are typically recommended during the follicular phase (after your period and before ovulation) for those actively trying to conceive. If you’re preparing your body for conception or doing a detox/reset, massage may be offered at other points in your cycle as advised by your provider.

🤍 Who is Fertility Massage For?

This treatment is perfect for those who:
  • Are trying to conceive naturally or through ART
  • Have experienced irregular cycles, PCOS, painful periods, blocked fallopian tubes or endometriosis
  • Want to reduce stress and connect with their body
  • Have a history of miscarriage or reproductive trauma
  • Are preparing their body for egg retrieval or embryo transfer

Ready to Begin Your Journey?

At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we combine clinical knowledge with compassionate care. Our massage therapists work hand-in-hand with our ABORM certified fertility acupuncture specialists to ensure your care is holistic and tailored to your needs.  

Book your Fertility Massage today and take the next step on your journey to motherhood

📞 Call us at 904-448-0046 or book online at www.hpjax.com

item.title: August is Pelvic Floor Dysfunction Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
Let’s Talk About the Foundation of Your Health

You know what they say… your pelvic floor is holding more than just your bladder. 💪 And in August, we’re shining a spotlight on one of the most under-discussed, misunderstood, and essential parts of your body: your pelvic floor. Pelvic Floor Dysfunction (PFD) affects millions of people—and yet, it often flies under the radar. Why? Because so many symptoms are dismissed as "normal," "just aging," or "something women just live with." We're here to say: no more. Over 33% of women and 16% of men are currently experiencing pelvic pain or dysfunction—and yet most have never been told what’s really going on. Let’s be clear: that is not normal. When it comes down to it, Pelvic Health is one of the most poorly understood—and therefore poorly managed—areas of medicine. But I’m here to change that. To truly understand pelvic health, we must start by understanding the pelvic floor and the vital role it plays in our daily function, comfort, and even our emotional wellbeing.

What Is the Pelvic Floor?

Simply put: your pelvic floor is part of your core system. It’s involved in nearly everything—and when it’s out of balance, so is your body. The pelvic floor is a group of muscles, ligaments, and tendons that form a kind of hammock or basket at the base of the pelvis. These muscles connect to the bones of the pelvis and help support the bladder, uterus, and bowels—keeping your abdominal organs in healthy alignment. These muscles play a vital role in:
  • Supporting your bladder, rectum, and reproductive organs
  • Managing urination and bowel movements
  • Providing stability for your spine and hips
  • Supporting healthy sexual function
  • Coordinating with your core for posture and balance
When these muscles are too weak, too tight, or not coordinating properly, we call it Pelvic Floor Dysfunction—and the ripple effects can be widespread.

Is Your Pelvic Floor Trying to Tell You Something?

Pelvic floor issues don’t just affect postpartum women or older adults. They can affect anyone—at any stage of life. If you’re experiencing any of these symptoms, your pelvic floor might need some love: ✨ Leaking when you cough, laugh, or jump ✨ Feeling heaviness or pressure “down there” ✨ Pain with intimacy or vaginal exams ✨ Constipation or straining to go ✨ Ongoing hip, low back, or tailbone pain And yet… so many of these symptoms are brushed aside, or misdiagnosed. That’s why awareness is key—because knowledge = power (and prevention).

How Acupuncture Can Help

Here at Health Pointe Jacksonville, we’ve helped hundreds of women (and men!) find relief through our pelvic floor acupuncture program. We address not just the symptoms—but the nervous system, fascia, and energy patterns that keep the pelvic floor locked in dysfunction.

 Acupuncture can help with:

  • Painful periods and endometriosis-related discomfort
  • Incontinence or overactive bladder
  • Constipation and pelvic congestion
  • Pain with intimacy
  • Postpartum recovery
  • Core coordination + nervous system regulation
We also work closely with other professionals when needed—such as pelvic floor physical therapists, OB-GYNs, or colorectal specialists—to make sure your care is comprehensive and collaborative.

Step Into Your Strength

Your pelvic floor isn’t just a set of muscles—it’s a foundation of support, pleasure, stability, and strength. Pelvic health is directly linked to your overall quality of life.   This August, let’s finally give pelvic health the attention it deserves.   If you’ve been feeling unheard or unsure of where to turn, know this: you’re not alone, and help is available. Whether you’re 6 weeks postpartum or 20 years into your menopause journey, it’s never too late to heal. We’re here when you're ready. Let’s get you back to feeling strong, supported, and connected. 📍Jacksonville, FL 💻 hpjax.com 📞 Call us at (904) 448-0046 🔗 Book a Consultation Today »

💡 Bonus Resources:

  • 📝 May is Pelvic Health Awareness Month – What You Need to Know
  • 🧠 Revolutionizing Pelvic Health: Introducing Our New Specialty Program

item.title: 🍂 November: A Season of Gratitude, Giving & Self Care
item.dc:creator: DoctorJulee
item.content:encoded:
As Jacksonville’s breeze cools and the pace of the year begins to settle, November reminds us to slow down, breathe deeply, and reflect on all we’ve been given — and all we have yet to give. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, this month is about gratitude in action: giving to our community, nurturing our bodies, and savoring the kind of laughter that comes from a full belly and a full heart. 💛

Don’t Forget You — Holiday Self-Care & Seasonal Tune-Ups

As we “fall back” with last weekend’s time change, our bodies begin adjusting to shorter days, cooler air, and shifting routines. That one-hour change may seem small, but it can disrupt sleep patterns, mood, digestion, and energy levels — especially during the busy holiday season. Now more than ever, self-care isn’t a luxury — it’s essential. Acupuncture seasonal tune-ups help your body adapt by calming the nervous system, balancing hormones, and improving sleep quality so you can show up grounded, focused, and joyful through the holidays. ✨ Take this as your reminder: don’t wait until you’re run down. Schedule your seasonal acupuncture tune-up today and give yourself the same care you give everyone else this time of year. Book Now → Feel balanced, rested, and ready for the season ahead.  Acupuncture Solutions for a Smooth Seasonal Transition Our specialized treatments offer gentle support to help you adapt by: – Balancing sleep-wake cycles through targeted meridian points – Regulating cortisol levels for effective stress management – Enhancing your body’s natural healing processes – Harmonizing yin and yang energies for overall well-being

Practitioner Tip: Schedule an acupuncture session during the first week of the time change to ease your body’s adjustment.

 Kindness in Action: Our Community Initiatives

🥫 Yes We CAN Make a Difference Food Drive

November 10–15, 2025 We’re partnering once again with the City Rescue Mission to help feed local families this Thanksgiving. Please drop off canned and non-perishable food items at the clinic — every single donation helps bring warmth and nourishment to someone’s table. Because no one should go hungry during a season of abundance.

🧸 HPJ Holiday Toy Drive & Raffle

11/24- 12/8/25 Help us make the holidays brighter for local children in need! Once again we’re collecting new, unwrapped toys for Toys For Tots donation, and for each toy donated, you’ll receive a raffle entry for amazing prizes. Your generosity helps make magic happen — both for the children receiving the toys and for you, as kindness has a beautiful way of giving back.

🥧 Pie Giveaway

November 24 & 25, 2025 Our favorite tradition returns! We’ll be giving away 50 delicious pumpkin or apple pies the Monday and Tuesday before Thanksgiving. First come, first served — stop by early to grab a pie and share a little sweetness with those you love.

💳 Annual Gift Card Sale

December 15–19, 2025 It’s our once-a-year event and the perfect opportunity to gift health and healing!  

🕯️ Holiday Hours

We’re giving our team a little time to recharge and enjoy family time this season:
  • Dec 23: Half Day (closing at 2 PM)
  • Dec 24–26: Closed for Christmas
  • Reopen Monday, Dec 29
  • Dec 31: Half Day (closing at 2 PM)
  • Jan 1: Closed for New Year’s Day
  • *Reopen Friday, Jan 2, 2026

Please schedule ahead to ensure you’re seen before the break — our calendar fills quickly this time of year!

💛 Full Hearts & Full-Belly Laughs

As the year closes, may your home be filled with gratitude, your table with warmth, and your days with laughter that echoes through every room. At Health Pointe Jacksonville, we’re endlessly thankful for each of you — for your trust, your stories, and your commitment to your health journey. You are the heart of our work, and we are honored to be part of yours. Here’s to full bellies, full hearts, and full-bodied laughter this holiday season — and to continuing our shared journey of healing and growth into the new year. With gratitude, Your HPJ Family
